title: Fix hyperbolic unit test
body:
The `1x-T4-4-17` instance type were missing in the latest catalog ([commit](https://github.com/skypilot-org/skypilot-catalog/commit/06cb4087855609446f3ce9739d863ffd92cdbcd6)), causing this unit test to fail, as it's explicitly looking for that type.

This PR fixes it by switching to a valid instance type which exist on the latest catalog.

We probably should still investigate though why `1x-T4-4-17` went missing.

Tested (run the relevant ones):

- [x] Code formatting: install pre-commit (auto-check on commit) or `bash format.sh`
- [x] Any manual or new tests for this PR (please specify below)
- [ ] All smoke tests: `/smoke-test` (CI) or `pytest tests/test_smoke.py` (local)
- [x] Relevant individual tests: `/smoke-test -k test_name` (CI) or `pytest tests/test_smoke.py::test_name` (local)
- [ ] Backward compatibility: `/quicktest-core` (CI) or `pytest tests/smoke_tests/test_backward_compat.py` (local)

<!-- CI commands (/-prefixed) can only be triggered by repo members -->

files_modified.patch:
@@ -53,8 +53,8 @@ def test_hyperbolic_resource_feasibility():
     """Test resource feasibility for Hyperbolic."""
     cloud = hyperbolic.Hyperbolic()
     resources = Resources(cloud=cloud,
-                          instance_type='1x-T4-4-17',
-                          accelerators={'T4': 1})
+                          instance_type='1x-H100-28-271',
+                          accelerators={'H100': 1})
     feasible = cloud._get_feasible_launchable_resources(resources)
     assert hasattr(feasible, 'resources_list')
     assert hasattr(feasible, 'fuzzy_candidate_list')

steps.operation: Update the Hyperbolic resource feasibility unit test to use an instance type and accelerator that exist in the latest catalog by replacing the hardcoded `1x-T4-4-17` / `T4` pair with `1x-H100-28-271` / `H100`.
steps.reason: The previous test data referenced an instance type (`1x-T4-4-17`) that was removed/missing from the latest skypilot-catalog, causing the test to fail despite correct feasibility logic. Switching to a known-valid instance type keeps the unit test aligned with the current catalog contents.
steps.side_effects: This changes the specific accelerator family covered by the test (T4 -> H100), which may reduce coverage for T4-related catalog parsing/feasibility paths; if other tests or fixtures assume T4 availability for Hyperbolic, they may now diverge from this test’s expectations.

step_plan.summary: Fixes a failing Hyperbolic unit test by updating the hardcoded instance type/accelerator pair to one that exists in the latest catalog, restoring test stability.
